title: BEM-921 worker request
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM921_SYSTEM_WORKING_PROTOCOL_V2_REQUEST.md with content # BEM-921 SYSTEM WORKING PROTOCOL V2 REQUEST
Status: SENT_TO_CURATOR
Route: External GPT auditor -> Curator -> Worker -> Curator -> external mailbox
Objective: prepare a new version of the full system improvement protocol to working state, incorporating all operator remarks from today and Claude APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS.
This must not be Telegram-only.
Mandatory updates from operator dialog:
1. Elements are not unique agents with local rules. Remove local_rule_refs/local_rules from the base model.
2. Element behavior = template role prompt + system rules + object rules + contour rules/properties + experience registry.
3. Curator is also a role and an element of the object, but not a member of an internal contour by default. Curator inherits system rules + object rules + channel rules, not contour rules.
4. Analyst/Auditor/Executor are elements inside internal contours and inherit contour rules/properties.
5. Workspace belongs to internal contour, not to each element. Main workspace is the repository's main code/state.
6. Every curator may have registered Telegram input/output; General Director curator remains primary top-level route.
7. Vertical communication is strictly curator-to-curator between objects.
8. Input to internal contour: curator -> analyst.
9. Internal contour algorithm is not Analyst -> Executor -> Auditor. Correct algorithm is iterative: analyst prepares proposal -> auditor reviews proposal -> if rejected return to analyst -> if approved pass to executor -> executor executes -> auditor reviews result -> if rejected return through analyst with revision proposal -> repeat until auditor-approved result.
10. Output from internal contour is auditor -> curator.
11. Horizontal contour communication inside an object is verified-data exchange: auditor of contour A sends to analyst of contour B, or analyst of B requests verified data from auditor of A.
12. GitHub Actions/workflows/runners are mechanisms that activate required roles/contours and write event_log/proof/status.
13. Every significant transition must create an event in event_log and relevant managed_channel/queue/result/proof files.
Claude mandatory requirements remain active:
- elements_registry.json, element prompts, objects_registry references elements registry, non-null commit_sha in release_proof_manifest from CI/Git, dispatch consumer auto-trigger, telegram input schema/mapping, managed channel consumer, BEM-925 DoD, mock/production E2E split, workflow concurrency groups, report_period_config version owner OPERATOR, BEM-923 secret names, BEM-928 checklist.
Required output:
- Protocol V2 full-system document.
- Detailed roadmap BEM-920..BEM-928 updated with all above requirements.
- Detailed prompt section: existing prompts, new prompts, prompt templates, and draft content/behavior for objects, curators, contours, analyst/auditor/executor roles.
- Tables for registries: elements_registry, contours_registry, rule_registry, experience_registry, report_period_config, telegram schemas, event log events.
- Clear operator gates and acceptance criteria.
Expected status: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES or BLOCKED_WITH_REASON.
No issue comments.
